title: Fagara bungei
taxonomy: Fagara bungei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a member of the genus Fagara, which includes several species of plants commonly known as prickly ash or Chinese pepper. The family Rutaceae comprises plants often referred to as citrus or rue family due to their similar botanical characteristics. Fagara bungei is native to China and other parts of East Asia. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rutaceae family.
uses: Fagara bungei, also known as Zanthoxylum bungeanum, is a plant species native to China and other parts of Asia. Its bark, seeds, and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for various purposes. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, indigestion, and other health issues. In traditional Chinese medicine, Fagara bungei is used to treat conditions such as sore throats, coughs, and respiratory problems. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. constituents: Fagara bungei contains active constituents such as limonoids, specifically nimbolin B and nimbin, which are known for their potential medicinal properties. These compounds are responsible for the plant's traditional uses in treating various ailments. In addition to limonoids, the plant also contains other bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. intro: Fagara bungei, also known as Zanthoxylum bungeanum or Sichuan pepper, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to China and other parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can help to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and other ailments. The bioactive constituents of Fagara bungei include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Fagara bungei is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and tinctures, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in traditional formulas.
